name: provekit-protocol
_unsigned: draft v1.1.0; minor bump for: (1) property memento role replaced by contract memento (pre/post/inv/outBinding); (2) implication memento role added (with Z3 witness body); (3) handshake algorithm spec added; (4) IR formal grammar maximal-uniformity cut (flat quantifiers, unified connective with operands, no sort on var/ctor, name everywhere); (5) cryptographic primitives are self-identifying — every hash and signature carries an algorithm tag inline; v1.1.0 ships with blake3-512 (hash) and ed25519 (signature/pubkey) as the only permitted tags. Migration to additional algorithms (post-quantum signatures, alternative hash functions) is additive: future catalogs add tags without invalidating any v1.1.0 bytes. Spec CIDs marked RECOMPUTE will be derived from the canonical bytes after the implementation cut lands; the catalog itself signs after that.
_breakingChanges: Property memento role removed; contract memento role added with body fields {pre?, post?, inv?, outBinding, preHash?, postHash?, invHash?, authoring}
Implication memento role added; minted by verifiers when Z3 discharges (Q implies P) at handshake time

IR-JSON shape: forall/exists are flat (no Lambda wrapper); and/or/not/implies use unified `operands` array; var and ctor terms drop `sort`; atomic uses `name` instead of `predicate`; `name` replaces `varName` everywhere

Cryptographic primitives are self-identifying: hashes are `blake3-512:<128-hex-char-digest>` (no truncation, no per-purpose lengths); signatures are `ed25519:<base64>`; pubkeys are `ed25519:<base64>`. The hex16/hex32 type distinction is removed; one regex governs all hashes
All previously-derived propertyHashes/CIDs are invalidated by the IR shape change AND by the hash widening; cross-language demos and PoCs re-derive everything
legacy-witness wrapper variant removed; producers MUST emit role-specific variants only
